key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $4939.7M less capital spending $168.7M ÷ 408.7M diluted shares per ADP/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250806_rpt20250630_acc0000008670_25_000037.htm
Employer Services retention + PEO mix shift; mid-single-digit revenue growth

open_questions: Confirm whether FY2025 client-fund interest ($489M) is peak-cycle or sustainable in base case.
Net debt ~$13/sh: refinancing and commercial paper rollover risk in stress scenario.
Maria Black proxy compensation alignment: full-tier DEF 14A review.
No approved third-party sources; re-scan when Substacks or fund letters added.
thesis: Automatic Data Processing is the largest payroll and human-capital-management processor in the United States, serving employers from small businesses to Fortune 500 companies. **[Fact]** FY2025 service revenue was **$20.6 billion** (+7% versus prior year), excluding **$75.2 billion** of PEO pass-through costs that flow through the balance sheet but are not owner cash. **[Fact]** The company processed payroll for tens of millions of workers globally and generated operating cash flow of **$4.9 billion** minus capital spending of **$0.17 billion** in FY2025. **[Fact]** Two reportable segments dominate: **Employer Services** (payroll, tax filing, time and benefits) and **PEO Services** (co-employment for mid-market clients). **[Inference]** ADP earns recurring fees per worksite employee plus interest spreads on client funds held between collection and disbursement.
why_market_wrong: The stock trades at a premium multiple typical of quality compounders while the market may underweight ADP's **client-fund interest spread** and **PEO mix shift**. **[Fact]** Interest on funds held for clients contributed **$489 million** of FY2025 segment revenue; rate cuts compress this high-margin line faster than payroll fees grow. **[Inference]** Conversely, the market may overweight **Paychex and cloud HCM competition** while underweighting payroll switching costs and ADP's compliance moat. **[Fact]** H1 FY2026 service revenue grew **6%** with diluted EPS up **9%**, suggesting retention remains solid. **[Inference]** No dated HK predictive attribute exists; the edge is **durable franchise at a fair price**, not a catalyst with a known payoff date.
